title: platform-user — cache keys + tags

keys.description: OnCallResolver::current() cache. Short TTL so a rotation change (someone offboarded mid-shift, PagerDuty updated its schedule) propagates within a minute. `department` is optional — omitted key resolves the platform-wide current shift.
invalidatedBy: PlatformUserObserver.updating (when on_call_rotation_key changed OR status changed)

keys.description: OnCallResolver::next() cache. Same shape as current shift but looks up the upcoming shift start.

Cached list of direct reports (compact references) for a given manager. Read on the platform-admin detail view. Invalidated on any PlatformUserManagerReassigned targeting this user OR any of their reports.
Materialised chain of managers for cycle detection + depth-cap enforcement in the observer's manager-reassignment gate.
invalidatedBy: PlatformUserObserver.updating (when manager_id changed on this row OR any ancestor)

keys.description: Cached result of the MfaCoverageProbe. Sampled every 5 minutes so a bad probe response does not stall readiness checks.
Active PlatformUser headcount by department. Feeds the `academorix.platform_user.active.count` metric + the ops dashboard.
invalidatedBy: PlatformUserObserver.updating (when status changed OR department changed)

keys.description: Onboarding checklist state for a pending PlatformUser. Cleared when the checklist reports complete OR the PlatformUser transitions out of `pending`.
Tracks time-since-PlatformUserOffboarded for the SLA probe. Cleared when RevokePlatformUserAccessJob completes.

notes: The on-call cache TTL is deliberately short (60s) despite the ops-critical use case — rotation changes must be visible quickly so a page does not go to an offboarded staffer.
The org-chart cache invalidation is CASCADED via tag purge: reassigning A’s manager purges both `platform-user:manager-chain:A` AND the reports cache of A's old + new managers.
There is deliberately NO cache of the PII-unmuted response — every viewPii call re-fetches so the meta-audit row is written on every access.

The `platform-user:mfa-coverage-ratio` cache is READ ONLY by the MfaCoverageProbe. It never affects the runtime request path (the `platform.mfa_required` middleware reads the live Identity row on every request).
